purpose: PHASE T3 reference inventory for PARBAUGHS dashboard token-meter redesign per dashboard-completion-spec-2026-05-15.md PRE-RESEARCH #13

key_observations: Three big-numeral stat cards in a row above a full-panel chart — canonical layout
Six independent filter axes (Workspaces, API keys, Models, Granularity, Period stepper, Group-by)
Cost and Usage are separate top-level views, not toggled within one panel
Per-model stacked-bar breakdown is the default chart treatment, not opt-in
Inline donut next to '$25.92 Total token cost' as compact distribution viz
Red border around 'Total web search cost $0.47' card draws attention to non-zero web-search spend (called out specifically)

vendors.context: API usage cost dashboard + billing settings
key_observations: OpenAI explicitly discloses dashboard lag of ~1 hour in docs — freshness stamp pattern
Cost and Activity are two views — same data, different lens
Help center pages 403 to WebFetch — direct console screenshots not accessible without auth
AVIF refs captured via saasframe.io aggregator pages — public marketing collation of console screenshots

vendors.context: Billing dashboard — MRR, churn, revenue recovery, rate cards
key_observations: Stripe pairs $112M revenue with both CY and PY values side-by-side (no arrow, just dual numbers) — alternative to delta-indicator
Monthly Revenue line chart overlays revenue + goal + volume series on shared y-axis
Declined Reason Breakdown pie chart with two large segments (41.7% / 58.3%) — categorical proportion viz pattern
Customer Growth bar chart uses categorical industry x-axis with single-bar-per-category

vendors.context: GitHub Actions billing + Advanced Security usage
key_observations: $2,569.90 big number with explainer text and View details CTA is reference-quality stat card
Time-frame dropdown + Group-by dropdown sit horizontally at top of every chart panel
Stacked area chart with 6 SKU lines + 'All other' bucket — canonical 5-plus-other categorical pattern
Per-organization breakdown table below chart with expandable rows (chevron) for drill-down
Table columns: Included / Billed / Gross / Billed-amount — exposes both count AND cost-after-credits
Tabs at top (Actions / Codespaces / Copilot / Advanced Security / Enterprise / Git LFS / Models / Packages) — product navigation

vendors.observed_state: text-only — pages are 403 to WebFetch or pure markdown without embedded screenshots
key_observations: Documented patterns extracted to notes.md — would benefit from live capture via Playwright if direct AWS console screenshots are required
AWS bakes in 'Month-to-date vs Last month same period' comparison with 'trend calculations might be influenced by number of days in month' caveat
Top Trends widget shows 'top 10 cost variations sorted by absolute dollar difference' with both % and absolute values
Cost summary widget specifically excludes credits/refunds — disclosed as a caveat on the widget
Cost Anomaly Detection 'Setup required' empty state with create-monitor CTA is reference empty-state pattern

vendors.observed_state: documentation-only — changelog references a video (light.mp4) but no static image URLs; docs pages list features without embedded screenshots; would require live capture
key_observations: Vercel explicitly names the allotment indicator pattern — current consumption + projected cost in one widget
Five lens types ('Count', 'Project', 'Region', 'Ratio', 'Average') applied to a single metric — multi-axis breakdown approach
Last-30-days as recommended default period selector value
Pro customers get differentiated dashboard treatment vs free tier — gating pattern
Spend Management and Usage Notifications are separate features — alerting is separate from visualization
Local dashboard purpose-built for Claude Code — closest analog to PARBAUGHS token meter use case
Pro/Max subscribers see progress bar against 5h/24h session quotas — non-subscribers see counter-only view
Session history is a primary affordance — not just totals but per-session drill-down
Large native screenshot (3840 x 4620) suggests a dense multi-section dashboard, not single-card

vendors.context: Documentation platform dashboard with usage statistics
API management dashboard with request metrics and usage tracking
Payment processing metrics and transaction overview
Financial dashboard with account balance and transaction analytics
Product analytics interface with performance indicators

gaps_for_live_capture.what: live Vercel usage dashboard with real data — current allotment indicator widget + project/region/ratio/average lens switcher
gaps_for_live_capture.why: Vercel only publishes mp4 video on changelog, no static screenshots; would require Playwright capture with authenticated session

gaps_for_live_capture.what: live OpenAI Platform usage dashboard at platform.openai.com/account/usage
gaps_for_live_capture.why: Help center articles 403 to WebFetch; AVIF refs are saasframe aggregator second-hand; direct capture preferred for high-fidelity reference

gaps_for_live_capture.what: live AWS Cost Explorer dashboard with daily-costs graph, top-trends widget, anomaly detection callout
gaps_for_live_capture.why: AWS docs are pure markdown without embedded screenshots; would benefit from authenticated console capture

gaps_for_live_capture.what: full enterprise billing landing page with all product tabs populated
gaps_for_live_capture.why: Captured 4 reference images cover individual chart panels but not the full landing-page composition

primary_relevance_to_parbaughs: Anthropic Console — strongest direct analog (same product family, same use case: developers tracking tokens)
phuryn/claude-usage — strongest functional analog (local dashboard, Claude-specific quota awareness)
GitHub Actions — strongest layout analog ($-headline + per-source breakdown + drill-down table)
Stripe — strongest design-craft analog (typography hierarchy, color discipline, sparkline integration)
